html:
[636]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 因曰 - Conversational phrase meaning 'then said' or 'thereupon said,' used to introduce dialogue.
  • 月白风清 - Classical phrase meaning 'the moon is bright and the wind is clear,' often used to describe a serene or picturesque night.
  • 如此良夜何 - Rhetorical question meaning 'what can we do with such a beautiful night?' It expresses a sense of appreciation or wonder.
  • 钟情何厚也 - Phrase meaning 'how deeply attached is he?' It suggests a question about the depth of someone's feelings.
  • 奇美特异者 - Phrase meaning 'those who are unusually beautiful or extraordinary,' referring to people or things that stand out.
  • 可以此诮东坡也 - Phrase meaning 'can we mock Su Shi with this?' It suggests a playful or critical remark about Su Shi (Su Dongpo).
  • 应彼此苦众矣 - Phrase meaning 'it should be a common experience for both of us,' implying that the situation described is not unique.
  • 于我何独无之 - Rhetorical question meaning 'why am I the only one without it?' It expresses a sense of shared experience or frustration.
  • 凡谓织女银河何在也 - Phrase meaning 'where is the Milky Way and the Weaver Girl?' It refers to a romantic or poetic question about the night sky.
  • 自失 - Phrase meaning 'to lose oneself,' often used to describe a state of confusion or distraction.
  • 未及即对 - Phrase meaning 'not having time to respond immediately,' indicating a moment of hesitation or surprise.
  • 俄闻户内岭问娇寝未 - Phrase meaning 'soon after, someone asked from inside the house if Jiao had gone to bed,' describing an interruption.
  • 遁去 - Verb meaning 'to flee' or 'to disappear,' often used to describe a sudden departure.
  • 追忆昨夕之事 - Phrase meaning 'to recall the events of last night,' indicating a reflection on past experiences.
  • 自疑有获 - Phrase meaning 'to doubt that one has gained something,' expressing a sense of uncertainty or skepticism.
  • 遇事多参商 - Phrase meaning 'to encounter many differences or disagreements,' suggesting frequent conflicts or misunderstandings.
  • 不自足 - Phrase meaning 'not feeling satisfied with oneself,' indicating a sense of inadequacy or dissatisfaction.
  • 作《减字木兰花》词以记之 - Phrase meaning 'to compose a poem titled 'Reduced Character Peony Flower' to record it,' describing the act of writing poetry as a form of memory or expression.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 东坡 - Refers to Su Shi (Su Dongpo), a famous poet and statesman of the Song Dynasty, known for his literary works and philosophical ideas.
  • 织女银河 - Refers to the mythological story of the Weaver Girl (Zhinü) and the Cowherd (Niulang), who are separated by the Milky Way. This is a well-known Chinese legend about love and separation.
  • 减字木兰花 - A poetic form, a variant of the 'Peony Flower' (Mu Lan Hua) poem, which is a traditional Chinese poetic genre known for its concise and expressive style.

Proper Nouns

  • 东坡 - Su Shi (Su Dongpo), a famous poet and statesman of the Song Dynasty.
  • 织女 - The Weaver Girl, a character from Chinese mythology.
  • 银河 - The Milky Way, a celestial phenomenon.
  • 减字木兰花 - A poetic form, a variant of the 'Peony Flower' (Mu Lan Hua) poem.

[637]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 春宵陪宴 - Refers to a spring evening spent attending a banquet.
  • 歌罢酒阑人正倦 - Classical phrase meaning 'after singing has ended and wine has been consumed, people are tired.'
  • 危坐中堂 - Means 'sitting upright in the central hall,' indicating a formal or respectful posture.
  • 倏见仙娥出洞房 - Poetic expression meaning 'suddenly seeing a fairy maiden emerge from the inner chamber.'
  • 博山香烬 - Refers to the burning incense in a mountain-shaped incense burner, symbolizing the fading of time or the end of an event.
  • 素手重添银漏永 - Means 'a delicate hand rekindles the silver water clock, prolonging the night.'
  • 织女斜河 - Refers to the Weaver Girl crossing the Milky Way, a reference to the Chinese legend of the Qixi Festival.
  • 月白凤清良夜何 - Poetic expression meaning 'the moon is bright, the phoenix is clear, what a good night it is.'
  • 生人揖妗 - Means 'the guest bows to the mother-in-law,' indicating a formal greeting.
  • 娇时对镜画眉未终 - Means 'Jiao is in front of the mirror, still drawing her eyebrows.'
  • 兰煤灯烬耶，烛花也 - Playful question meaning 'Is the lamp coal burned out, or is it just the candle flower?'
  • 既许君矣，宁惜此 - Means 'Since I have already agreed to you, how can I be stingy with this?'
  • 缘兄得此，可作无事人那 - Playful question meaning 'Since you have this, can you now be a carefree person?'
  • 敢不留以为贽 - Means 'How could I not keep it as a token?'
  • 子宜分以遗我，何重劳客耶 - Means 'You should divide it and give it to me, why make the guest work hard?'
  • 遂以指决煤之半以赠生 - Means 'She then divided the coal with her finger and gave half to the guest.'
  • 因牵生衣拭指污处 - Means 'She then pulled the guest's robe to wipe the dirty spot on her finger.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 春宵陪宴 - Refers to a spring evening banquet, a common social event in classical Chinese literature.
  • 仙娥出洞房 - A reference to a fairy maiden emerging from an inner chamber, evoking a sense of mystery and beauty.
  • 博山香烬 - Refers to a mountain-shaped incense burner, symbolizing the passage of time or the end of an event.
  • 银漏永 - Refers to a water clock, a device used to measure time, symbolizing the prolonging of the night.
  • 织女斜河 - Refers to the Chinese legend of the Weaver Girl (Zhinü) crossing the Milky Way (He), a story associated with the Qixi Festival.
  • 月白凤清良夜何 - A poetic expression evoking a beautiful and serene night, with the moon and phoenix symbolizing purity and grace.

Proper Nouns

  • 生 - Refers to the guest or protagonist in the narrative.
  • 娇 - Refers to a female character, likely the wife or daughter of the host.
  • 妗 - Refers to the mother-in-law of the host, a key figure in the narrative.

[638]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 積久方成 - Means 'accumulated over time to become' or 'formed gradually over a long period.'
  • 油污不堪自整 - Means 'soiled with oil, unable to clean oneself,' suggesting a state of disarray or messiness.
  • 花淚盈暈 - A metaphor for the tears of the flower, suggesting a poignant or sorrowful quality.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 蘭煤燈 - Refers to an ancient type of lamp, often used in literary contexts to evoke a sense of intimacy or romance.
  • 佳人 - A common literary term for a beautiful woman, often used in poetry and prose to describe a beloved or idealized figure.
  • 暮春 - Refers to the late spring season, a time often associated with renewal and the blooming of flowers.
  • 梨花 - Pear blossoms, often used in Chinese literature to symbolize beauty and purity.
  • 東皇 - Often associated with spring, symbolizing renewal and growth.

Proper Nouns

  • 蘭煤燈 - A type of lamp.
  • 佳人 - A beautiful woman.
  • 東皇 - Often associated with spring, symbolizing renewal and growth.
  • 梨花 - Pear blossoms, a symbol of beauty and purity.

[639]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 我自遇子之后，魂飞魄扬，不能着体，夜更苦长，竟夕不寐 - Describes the speaker's emotional state after meeting the other person, with a focus on restlessness and insomnia.
  • 汝方以为戏，足见子之心也 - Contrasts the other person's dismissive attitude with the speaker's perception of their true feelings.
  • 予每见子言语态度，非无情者，及予言深情味，则子变色以拒我 - Describes the speaker's observation of the other person's emotional reactions, highlighting a defensive response.
  • 岂可不解世事，而为是沽矫哉 - Questions the other person's understanding of worldly affairs and their insincere behavior.
  • 谅孱缪之迹，不足以当雅意，深藏自闭，将有售也 - Expresses the speaker's self-deprecation and the reason for their withdrawal.
  • 今日一言之后，余将西骑矣 - Indicates the speaker's decision to leave after the conversation.
  • 子无苦戏我 - A plea not to be taken lightly.
  • 娇因慨然良久 - Describes Jiao's emotional response.
  • 君疑妾矣，妾敢无言 - Expresses Jiao's desire to clarify the situation.
  • 妾知兄心久矣 - Indicates Jiao's deep understanding of the other person's feelings.
  • 岂敢固自郑重以要君也 - Suggests that Jiao's actions are sincere.
  • 第恐不能终始，其如后患何 - Expresses concern about the future.
  • 妾亦数月来诸事不复措意，寝梦不安，饮食俱废 - Describes Jiao's state of distress.
  • 君所不得知也 - Indicates that the speaker's condition is unknown to the other person.
  • 君疑甚矣，异日之事，君任之 - Expresses a commitment to the other person.
  • 果不济，当以死谢君 - Indicates a strong commitment.
  • 子果有志，则以策我 - Suggests mutual support.
  • 因长吁曰 - Indicates a moment of reflection.
  • 生曰 - Indicates a response from the male character.
  • 娇未及答，俄然舅自外至 - Indicates an interruption.
  • 生因起出迎舅 - Describes a polite action.
  • 娇乃反室，不可再语 - Indicates a withdrawal from conversation.
  • 生乃赋《石州引》词，以记其事云 - Describes a literary response.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 《石州引》 - A specific type of Chinese poetry, likely a ci (a form of lyric poetry) with a specific tune or rhythm.
  • 西骑 - Refers to traveling westward, possibly indicating a journey or a departure.
  • 娇 - Likely a female character's name, suggesting a personal story.
  • 舅 - Refers to the uncle, indicating a familial relationship.
  • 夜更苦长，竟夕不寐 - A common theme in classical Chinese literature, reflecting the emotional turmoil and insomnia experienced by characters.
  • 魂飞魄扬 - A metaphor often used in classical Chinese literature to describe a state of intense emotion or spiritual disturbance.
  • 变色以拒我 - A phrase indicating a strong emotional reaction, often used to describe a defensive or angry response.
  • 深藏自闭 - A metaphorical expression indicating a state of isolation or withdrawal, often used to describe a character's inner turmoil.
  • 数月来诸事不复措意 - A phrase indicating a period of neglect or distraction, often used to describe a character's emotional state.
  • 寝梦不安，饮食俱废 - A phrase indicating a state of distress, often used to describe a character's physical and mental health.
  • 异日之事，君任之 - A phrase indicating a commitment to the future, often used to express trust and responsibility.
  • 果不济，当以死谢君 - A phrase indicating a strong commitment, often used to express loyalty and dedication.
  • 子果有志，则以策我 - A phrase indicating mutual support, often used to express encouragement and partnership.

Proper Nouns

  • 娇 - The name of the female character.
  • 舅 - The name of the uncle.
  • 《石州引》 - The title of the poem composed by the male character.

[640]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 懊恨 - Compound meaning 'grief and resentment,' expressing deep emotional distress.
  • 东君 - Poetic term for the 'Lord of the East' or 'Spring God,' often used metaphorically to represent spring or the season.
  • 催趱 - Compound meaning 'to urge forward' or 'to hasten,' often used in the context of time or progress.
  • 春意牢落 - Compound meaning 'spring's desolate mood' or 'spring's melancholy,' describing a sense of sadness or emptiness in spring.
  • 梨花粉泪溶溶 - Poetic description of 'pear blossoms shedding tears like powder,' symbolizing sorrow or beauty in decay.
  • 冲寒向晚 - Compound meaning 'braving the cold towards evening,' describing a journey or action taken in adverse conditions.
  • 佳人无语从地掷 - Poetic phrase meaning 'a beautiful woman silently discards something from the ground,' often interpreted as a metaphor for unspoken emotions or loss.
  • 瞥见却惊猜 - Compound meaning 'to glance and be startled in suspicion,' describing a moment of surprise and doubt.
  • 忍使芳尘歇 - Poetic phrase meaning 'how can one let the fragrant dust rest,' symbolizing the reluctance to let beauty or goodness fade.
  • 收拾道明窗净几 - Compound meaning 'to tidy up and arrange a bright window and clean desk,' describing preparation or order.
  • 瓶里一枝 - Simple phrase meaning 'a single branch in a vase,' often used to describe a small, delicate object.
  • 便添风月 - Compound meaning 'to add to the beauty of the wind and moon,' symbolizing the enhancement of natural beauty or atmosphere.
  • 因念多才 - Compound meaning 'thinking of many talents,' often used to describe admiration for someone's abilities.
  • 值此苦寒时节 - Compound meaning 'in this bitterly cold season,' describing a difficult or harsh time.
  • 近新消减 - Compound meaning 'recently diminished' or 'recently weakened,' describing a decline in strength or vitality.
  • 料有万斛春愁 - Poetic phrase meaning 'likely to have a thousand measures of spring sorrow,' symbolizing deep, overwhelming sadness.
  • 芭蕉未展丁香结 - Poetic phrase meaning 'the banana leaves not yet unfurled and the jasmine buds not yet opened,' symbolizing unfulfilled potential or hidden emotions.
  • 甚日把山盟向枕边说 - Poetic phrase meaning 'when will one speak of the mountain vows by the pillow,' symbolizing a promise or commitment that is yet to be fulfilled.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 东君 - Poetic term for the 'Lord of the East' or 'Spring God,' often used metaphorically to represent spring or the season.
  • 春意牢落 - Poetic expression describing a sense of sadness or emptiness in spring, often used to convey melancholy or unfulfilled expectations.
  • 梨花粉泪溶溶 - Poetic description of 'pear blossoms shedding tears like powder,' symbolizing sorrow or beauty in decay, often used in classical poetry to evoke a sense of loss or transience.
  • 佳人无语从地掷 - Poetic phrase meaning 'a beautiful woman silently discards something from the ground,' often interpreted as a metaphor for unspoken emotions or loss, reflecting the emotional depth of classical Chinese poetry.
  • 瞥见却惊猜 - Poetic phrase meaning 'to glance and be startled in suspicion,' describing a moment of surprise and doubt, often used to convey the complexity of human emotions.
  • 忍使芳尘歇 - Poetic phrase meaning 'how can one let the fragrant dust rest,' symbolizing the reluctance to let beauty or goodness fade, often used to express a desire to preserve beauty or virtue.
  • 收拾道明窗净几 - Poetic phrase meaning 'to tidy up and arrange a bright window and clean desk,' describing preparation or order, often used to convey a sense of readiness or intention.
  • 瓶里一枝 - Simple phrase meaning 'a single branch in a vase,' often used to describe a small, delicate object, symbolizing the beauty of simplicity or the transient nature of life.
  • 便添风月 - Poetic phrase meaning 'to add to the beauty of the wind and moon,' symbolizing the enhancement of natural beauty or atmosphere, often used to describe the enrichment of life or experience.
  • 因念多才 - Poetic phrase meaning 'thinking of many talents,' often used to describe admiration for someone's abilities, reflecting the classical Chinese appreciation for talent and virtue.
  • 值此苦寒时节 - Poetic phrase meaning 'in this bitterly cold season,' describing a difficult or harsh time, often used to convey the challenges of life or the passage of time.
  • 近新消减 - Poetic phrase meaning 'recently diminished' or 'recently weakened,' describing a decline in strength or vitality, often used to express the passage of time or the loss of youth.
  • 料有万斛春愁 - Poetic phrase meaning 'likely to have a thousand measures of spring sorrow,' symbolizing deep, overwhelming sadness, often used to convey the emotional weight of unfulfilled desires or lost love.
  • 芭蕉未展丁香结 - Poetic phrase meaning 'the banana leaves not yet unfurled and the jasmine buds not yet opened,' symbolizing unfulfilled potential or hidden emotions, often used to describe the complexity of human feelings or the passage of time.
  • 甚日把山盟向枕边说 - Poetic phrase meaning 'when will one speak of the mountain vows by the pillow,' symbolizing a promise or commitment that is yet to be fulfilled, often used to convey the longing for a future that has not yet come.

Proper Nouns

  • No proper nouns in the passage.

[642]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 曉窗寂寂驚相遇 - In the quiet dawn, a surprising encounter; emphasizes serendipity and tranquility.
  • 欲把芳心深意訴 - To express one's tender feelings and deep thoughts; highlights emotional depth.
  • 低眉敛翠不胜春 - Lowering the eyebrows and hiding the beauty, unable to bear the springtime; suggests modesty and shyness.
  • 娇转樱唇红半吐 - The rosy lips of the maiden turn slightly, revealing a hint of red; emphasizes subtle beauty and charm.
  • 匆匆已约欢娱处 - Hurriedly agreed upon a place for pleasure; suggests rushed excitement.
  • 可恨无情连夜雨 - Regrettable is the rain that falls all night, showing no mercy; emphasizes disappointment and frustration.
  • 枕孤裳冷不成眠 - Lying alone with cold bedding, unable to sleep; highlights loneliness and restlessness.
  • 挑尽残灯天未曙 - Stirring the dying lamp until dawn breaks; emphasizes a long and sleepless night.
  • 生晨起会娇于妗所 - The man rose early to meet the maiden at his aunt's place; suggests urgency and importance.
  • 因共至中堂，以夜所缀词示之 - They went together to the main hall, where he showed her the poem he had composed the previous night; emphasizes the significance of the poem.
  • 好事多磨 - Good things often come through trials; highlights challenges and difficulties in their relationship.
  • 然妾既许君矣，当别图之 - But since I have already promised you, we should think of another plan; suggests a practical approach to their situation.
  • 生剪缕发，书盟言于片纸付娇，娇亦剪发设盟以复于生 - The man cut a lock of his hair, wrote a pledge on a piece of paper, and gave it to the maiden; the maiden also cut a lock of her hair and set a pledge to return to the man; emphasizes the seriousness and commitment of their promise.
  • 虽是极意慕恋，然终于无便可乘 - Although they deeply yearned for each other, there was no way to act on their feelings; highlights frustration and helplessness.
  • 娇顾恋之极，作诗送行 - The maiden, deeply attached, wrote a poem to send him off; emphasizes emotional depth and farewell.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 妗所 - Refers to the aunt's place, a common setting for meetings and social gatherings in traditional Chinese families.
  • 中堂 - Refers to the main hall of a house, often used for important family gatherings and ceremonies.
  • 盟誓 - Refers to a formal oath or pledge, often involving cutting hair or other symbolic acts to seal a promise.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • The phrase '曉窗寂寂驚相遇' can be interpreted as 'In the quiet dawn, a surprising encounter,' emphasizing the serendipity and tranquility of the meeting.
  • The phrase '欲把芳心深意訴' can be read as 'to express one's tender feelings and deep thoughts,' highlighting the emotional depth of the interaction.
  • The phrase '低眉敛翠不胜春' can be interpreted as 'lowering the eyebrows and hiding the beauty, unable to bear the springtime,' suggesting a sense of shyness and modesty.
  • The phrase '娇转樱唇红半吐' can be read as 'the rosy lips of the maiden turn slightly, revealing a hint of red,' emphasizing the maiden's subtle beauty and charm.
  • The phrase '匆匆已约欢娱处' can be interpreted as 'hurriedly agreed upon a place for pleasure,' suggesting a rushed and excited agreement.
  • The phrase '可恨无情连夜雨' can be read as 'regrettable is the rain that falls all night, showing no mercy,' emphasizing the disappointment and frustration caused by the rain.
  • The phrase '枕孤裳冷不成眠' can be interpreted as 'lying alone with cold bedding, unable to sleep,' highlighting the loneliness and restlessness of the night.
  • The phrase '挑尽残灯天未曙' can be read as 'stirring the dying lamp until dawn breaks,' emphasizing the long and sleepless night.
  • The phrase '生晨起会娇于妗所' can be interpreted as 'the man rose early to meet the maiden at his aunt's place,' suggesting a sense of urgency and importance.
  • The phrase '因共至中堂，以夜所缀词示之' can be read as 'they went together to the main hall, where he showed her the poem he had composed the previous night,' emphasizing the significance of the poem.
  • The phrase '好事多磨' can be interpreted as 'good things often come through trials,' highlighting the challenges and difficulties in their relationship.
  • The phrase '然妾既许君矣，当别图之' can be read as 'but since I have already promised you, we should think of another plan,' suggesting a practical approach to their situation.
  • The phrase '生剪缕发，书盟言于片纸付娇，娇亦剪发设盟以复于生' can be interpreted as 'the man cut a lock of his hair, wrote a pledge on a piece of paper, and gave it to the maiden; the maiden also cut a lock of her hair and set a pledge to return to the man,' emphasizing the seriousness and commitment of their promise.
  • The phrase '虽是极意慕恋，然终于无便可乘' can be read as 'although they deeply yearned for each other, there was no way to act on their feelings,' highlighting the frustration and helplessness of their situation.
  • The phrase '娇顾恋之极，作诗送行' can be interpreted as 'the maiden, deeply attached, wrote a poem to send him off,' emphasizing the emotional depth and farewell.

[643]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 绿叶阴浓 - Describes dense green foliage, often used metaphorically to evoke lushness or abundance.
  • 声声杜字劝春归 - Literally 'the sound of the cuckoo bird urging spring to return,' a common poetic expression for the arrival of spring or the passage of time.
  • 相如千里悠悠去 - Refers to the legendary tale of Sima Xiangru, a famous poet and musician, who traveled far from his beloved Zhuo Wenjun. '悠悠' conveys a sense of long, lingering departure.
  • 不道文君泪湿衣 - '不道' means 'did not say' or 'did not tell,' but in this context, it implies that Zhuo Wenjun did not express her sorrow. '泪湿衣' literally means 'tears wet her clothes,' a metaphor for deep emotional distress.
  • 生得诗和韵以复娇 - '生得' means 'composed' or 'wrote,' and '诗和韵' refers to a poem with rhythm and rhyme. '以复娇' suggests that the poem was written to respond to or comfort someone (likely Zhuo Wenjun).
  • 密幄重帏舞蝶稀 - '密幄重帏' describes a dimly lit, secluded room, while '舞蝶稀' means 'few dancing butterflies,' evoking a sense of loneliness or isolation.
  • 殷勤分付东园柳 - '殷勤' means 'diligent' or 'sincere,' and '分付' means 'entrust' or 'instruct.' '东园柳' refers to the willow trees in the eastern garden, often symbolic of farewell or longing.
  • 只恐重来绿成阴也 - '只恐' means 'fear' or 'worry,' and '重来' means 'to come again.' '绿成阴' means 'green leaves become dense,' suggesting that time has passed and the willow trees have grown.
  • 青梅如豆，辜负梁州，恨悠悠 - '青梅如豆' describes young green plums, a symbol of youth. '辜负' means 'to辜负' or 'to fail to live up to,' and '梁州' refers to a place, possibly a metaphor for a lost opportunity or a distant lover. '恨悠悠' means 'endless regret' or 'endless sorrow.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 小梁州 - A type of Chinese folk song or tune, often used in poetry to express longing or separation.
  • 卜算子 - A traditional Chinese poetic form, often used to express personal feelings or emotions.

Proper Nouns

  • 小梁州 - The name of the poetic form or tune.
  • 卜算子 - The name of the poetic form.

[644]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 娇情不自已 - Idiomatic expression meaning 'the affectionate feelings cannot be restrained.' Describes the emotional state of the protagonist who is unable to control his feelings.
  • 复继以诗云 - Idiomatic expression meaning 'then followed with a poem saying.' Indicates that after the emotional outburst, the protagonist composed a poem to express his feelings further.
  • 临别殷勤诗语长 - Idiomatic expression meaning 'at the moment of parting, the affectionate and earnest words in the poem are long.' Emphasizes the depth and length of the emotional expression in the poem.
  • 云云去后早还乡 - Idiomatic expression meaning 'after you leave, return home early.' Suggests a wish for the beloved to return soon.
  • 小楼记取梅花约 - Idiomatic expression meaning 'remember the plum blossom appointment at the small pavilion.' Refers to a promise or agreement made at a specific place, symbolizing a cherished memory.
  • 目断江山几夕阳 - Idiomatic expression meaning 'the eyes can see the sunset over the mountains and rivers for a few times.' Conveys a sense of longing and the fleeting nature of time.
  • 自后生从父以他故不果行 - Idiomatic expression meaning 'afterwards, the young man followed his father for other reasons and did not fulfill the journey.' Explains the reason why the protagonist did not go as planned.
  • 行住坐卧，饮食起居 - Idiomatic expression meaning 'walking, standing, sitting, sleeping, eating, drinking, and daily routines.' Lists various aspects of daily life.
  • 无非为娇兴念 - Idiomatic expression meaning 'all are for the sake of thinking of the beloved.' Indicates that every action and thought is centered around the beloved.
  • 沉思成病 - Idiomatic expression meaning 'deep thoughts lead to illness.' Describes the physical manifestation of emotional distress.
  • 托求医 - Idiomatic expression meaning 'seeking medical help as an excuse.' Shows that the protagonist uses illness as a pretext to visit the beloved's family.
  • 舅妗为之皇皇 - Idiomatic expression meaning 'the aunt and uncle are greatly worried.' Describes the concern of the beloved's relatives.
  • 医卜踵至 - Idiomatic expression meaning 'doctors and fortune-tellers came one after another.' Indicates that many people visited to help the protagonist.
  • 但云生功名失意，劳思所致 - Idiomatic expression meaning 'they only say that the protagonist's failure in career and hard thoughts caused it.' Explains the perceived cause of the protagonist's illness.
  • 数日，病小愈 - Idiomatic expression meaning 'after several days, the illness slightly improved.' Indicates a temporary recovery.
  • 一口，舅出报谒 - Idiomatic expression meaning 'one day, the uncle went out to report and greet.' Describes the uncle's action.

  • 夏暑将残，秋风又动 - A description of the changing seasons, symbolizing the passage of time and the transition from one phase to another.
  • 忽收萧夫人及二兄书，取生回，应乡试 - A phrase indicating the sudden receipt of letters from a woman (萧夫人) and two brothers, summoning the protagonist back home for the provincial examination.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 遂謂生曰 - 遂 means 'thus' or 'then,' and 謂 means 'to say' or 'to address.' This phrase sets up a direct speech scenario.
  • 正爾喜歡 - 正爾 means 'just as you are' or 'as you are,' and 喜歡 means 'happy' or 'pleased.' Together, it conveys a sense of being content or satisfied.
  • 乃有遠別 - 乃 means 'then' or 'thereupon,' and 遠別 means 'a distant farewell.' This phrase indicates a significant separation.
  • 天耶、人耶 - 耶 is a particle used to form a question, similar to 'is it' or 'are they.' This phrase questions whether the cause of the separation is divine or human.
  • 何至此極也 - 何 means 'why,' 至 means 'to,' and 此極 means 'this extreme.' This phrase asks why things have reached such an extreme state.
  • 我為母兄所逼 - 為...所逼 means 'to be forced by...,' indicating that the speaker is under pressure from their mother and elder brother.
  • 只暫歸 - 只 means 'only' or 'just,' and 暫歸 means 'a temporary return.' This phrase indicates a short visit home.
  • 三兩月間再圖相見 - 三兩月 means 'three or two months,' and 再圖相見 means 'to plan to meet again.' This phrase suggests a future meeting after a short period.
  • 子第寬心 - 子 is a respectful term for the listener, 第 means 'please,' and 寬心 means 'be at ease.' This phrase advises the listener to remain calm.
  • 保圖眠食 - 保圖 means 'preserve' or 'maintain,' and 眠食 means 'sleep and food.' This phrase encourages the listener to take care of themselves.
  • 勿為亡益之悲 - 勿 means 'do not,' 為 means 'for,' 亡益 means 'no benefit,' and 悲 means 'sorrow.' This phrase advises against unnecessary grief.
  • 徒損傾國之貌 - 徒 means 'only' or 'in vain,' 損 means 'damage,' 傾國 means 'a nation's beauty,' and 貌 means 'appearance.' This phrase warns against damaging one's beauty.
  • 嫂掩泣曰 - 嫂 means 'sister-in-law,' 掩泣 means 'to wipe tears,' and 曰 means 'said.' This phrase describes the sister-in-law's emotional state.
  • 兄途中謹慎 - 兄 means 'elder brother,' 途中 means 'on the journey,' and 謹慎 means 'cautious.' This phrase advises the elder brother to be careful during his journey.
  • 早早到家 - 早早 means 'early' or 'soon,' and 到家 means 'to arrive home.' This phrase encourages the elder brother to return home quickly.
  • 有便即來 - 有便 means 'when there is an opportunity,' and 即來 means 'come immediately.' This phrase suggests that the listener should come as soon as possible.
  • 勿為長往 - 勿 means 'do not,' 為 means 'for,' 長往 means 'a long absence.' This phrase advises against a prolonged separation.
  • 妾醜陋之身 - 妾 means 'I' (a humble term for a woman), 醜陋 means 'ugly,' and 之身 means 'my body.' This phrase expresses the speaker's self-deprecation.
  • 乃兄所有 - 乃 means 'your,' 兄 means 'elder brother,' and 所有 means 'belong to.' This phrase indicates that the speaker belongs to the elder brother.
  • 倘念日盟 - 倘 means 'if,' 念 means 'remember,' 日 means 'daily,' and 盟 means 'vows.' This phrase suggests remembering daily vows.
  • 不我遐棄 - 不 means 'do not,' 我 means 'me,' 遐 means 'far,' and 弃 means 'abandon.' This phrase means 'do not abandon me far away.'
  • 雖死之日，猶生之年 - 雖 means 'even if,' 死之日 means 'the day of death,' 猶 means 'still,' and 生之年 means 'the year of life.' This phrase expresses the idea that even on the day of death, the speaker still feels alive.
  • 乃向生再拜曰 - 乃 means 'then,' 向 means 'to,' 生 means 'you' (a respectful term), 再拜 means 'to bow twice,' and 曰 means 'said.' This phrase describes the speaker's respectful greeting.
  • 只此別兄，明日不能出矣 - 只此 means 'just this,' 別兄 means 'farewell to you,' 明日 means 'tomorrow,' 不能 means 'cannot,' and 出 means 'go out.' This phrase indicates that the speaker will not be able to leave tomorrow.
  • 生亦咽埂 - 生 means 'you' (a respectful term), 亦 means 'also,' 嚎埂 means 'choking' or 'sobbing.' This phrase describes the speaker's emotional state.
  • 目送娉還 - 目送 means 'to watch with one's eyes,' 娉 means 'beautiful' or 'graceful,' and 還 means 'return.' This phrase describes the speaker watching the other person return.
  • 次日，娉又遣福福叩門 - 次日 means 'the next day,' 娉 means 'beautiful' or 'graceful,' 又 means 'again,' 遣 means 'send,' 福福 is a name, and 叩門 means 'knock on the door.' This phrase describes the next day's events.
  • 持手簡送青絲履一雙，綾襪一贈生 - 持 means 'hold,' 手簡 means 'a letter,' 送 means 'send,' 青絲履 means 'black silk shoes,' 一雙 means 'a pair,' 綾襪 means 'silk stockings,' 一 means 'one,' and 贈 means 'give.' This phrase describes the gift being sent.
  • 簡云 - 簡 means 'letter,' and 云 means 'says.' This phrase introduces the content of the letter.
  • 薄命妾婢再拜寓言兄前 - 薄命 means 'fateless' or 'unlucky,' 妾婢 means 'a lowly woman,' 再拜 means 'to bow twice,' 寓言 means 'a metaphor,' and 兄前 means 'before you.' This phrase is a formal greeting.
  • 媳薄命不得奉侍左右 - 媳 means 'wife,' 薄命 means 'fateless' or 'unlucky,' 不得 means 'cannot,' 奉侍 means 'serve,' and 左右 means 'at your side.' This phrase expresses the wife's inability to serve her husband.
  • 為久計 - 為 means 'for,' 久 means 'long,' and 計 means 'plan.' This phrase suggests a long-term plan.
  • 今馬首欲東 - 今 means 'now,' 馬首 means 'the head of the horse,' and 欲東 means 'wants to go east.' This phrase indicates the direction of travel.
  • 無可相贈 - 無 means 'no,' 可 means 'can,' and 相贈 means 'give to each other.' This phrase means there is nothing to give.
  • 手制粗鞋一雙 - 手制 means 'handmade,' 粗鞋 means 'coarse shoes,' and 一雙 means 'a pair.' This phrase describes the handmade shoes.
  • 絞襪一綱 - 絞襪 means 'hand-knitted socks,' and 一綱 means 'one piece.' This phrase describes the hand-knitted socks.
  • 聊表微意 - 聊 means 'just,' 表 means 'express,' and 微意 means 'small intention.' This phrase means to express a small intention.
  • 庶步履所至，猶妾之在足下也 - 庶 means 'perhaps,' 步履 means 'steps,' 所至 means 'wherever they go,' 猶 means 'still,' 妾 means 'I' (a humble term for a woman), and 在足下 means 'under your feet.' This phrase suggests that wherever the steps go, the speaker is still under the listener's feet.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • The use of '妾' and '兄' reflects traditional Chinese familial and social hierarchies, where '妾' is a humble term for a woman, and '兄' is a respectful term for an elder brother.

  • 斜香云倚翠屏，纱衣先觉露华零 - Couplet describing a scene where fragrant clouds lean against a green screen, and the poet feels the dew on their silk robe.
  • 谁云天上无离合，看取牵牛织女星 - Couplet referring to the myth of the Cowherd and the Weaver Girl, suggesting that even in heaven, there are moments of separation and reunion.
  • 流云不动鹊飞多，微步香尘袜罗 - Couplet describing a scene where clouds are still but magpies are flying, and the poet takes small steps in fragrant dust.
  • 若道神仙无配偶，怎教织女渡银河 - Couplet suggesting that if immortals do not have partners, how can the Weaver Girl cross the Milky Way?
  • 娟娟新月照围屏，井上梧桐一叶零 - Couplet describing a new moon shining on a screen and a single leaf falling from a tree by a well.
  • 今夕不知何夕也，双星错道是三星 - Couplet suggesting that the poet does not know what night it is, and the two stars seem to be mistaken for three.
  • 何期好事多乖，会难离易 - Couplet expressing the poet's disappointment that good things often go wrong, and that parting is easier than meeting.
  • 次早，生收家报母讣音，竟不及荣上提举之任，而丁忧之行逼矣 - Sentence describing the poet receiving news of his mother's death and being unable to take up his official post due to the need to observe mourning.
  • 夫人乃召边妪告之曰 - Sentence describing the poet's wife summoning an elderly woman to speak to her.

  • 吾止有此一息，时刻不面，尚且念之 - Sentence describing the wife's concern about being separated from her son.

  • 美人何在，忍看残镜，忍看残块 - Rhetorical question and two parallel clauses meaning 'Where is the beautiful person? Can one bear to look at the broken mirror, can one bear to look at the broken pieces?' Conveys the pain of loss and the inability to face reminders of the past.

  • 微启朱唇，耳畔低声儿说 - Tender and intimate moment, meaning 'Gently opening red lips, whispering softly in the ear.'
  • 冥君许我还魂也，教我同心，罗带重结 - Supernatural intervention, meaning 'The underworld god allows me to return to life, instructing me to be united, and to tie the silk sash again.'
  • 醒来惊怪，还疑又信，枕寒灯灭 - Mix of disbelief and acceptance upon waking, meaning 'Awakening in surprise and wonder, still doubting yet believing, with a cold pillow and extinguished lamp.'
  • 生到任不觉雪花飘粉，梅蕊舒琼，兔走乌飞，又当腊月 - Poetic description of the passage of time, meaning 'Not noticing the snowflakes falling like powder, the plum blossoms blooming, the moon and sun racing, it is already December.'
  • 有长安丞宋子璧者，一室女年及笄，姿艳绝世，忽暴死，已三日，复苏，不认其父母 - Detailed account of a miraculous event, meaning 'There was a county magistrate named Song Zibei in Chang'an, whose daughter, at the age of marriage, was exceptionally beautiful, suddenly died violently, remained dead for three days, and then revived, not recognizing her parents.'
  • 我贾平章女云华，今咸宁县宣差贾麟姊也 - Declaration of identity, meaning 'I am the daughter of Chancellor Jia, Yunhua, and the sister of Jia Lin, the magistrate of Xianning County.'
  • 死已二年，数当还魂 - Statement of the supernatural, meaning 'I have been dead for two years, and now it is time for me to return to life.'
  • 盖咸宁与长安，俱西安在城属县，廨宇相邻 - Geographical description, meaning 'Xianning and Chang'an are both counties under the jurisdiction of Xi'an, with their government offices adjacent.'
  • 丞亦闻贾尹到任时，其姊氏亡故 - Historical reference, meaning 'The magistrate also heard that when Chancellor Jia took office, his sister had died.'
  • 还魂之事，世所罕有 - Statement of rarity, meaning 'The return to life is a rare occurrence in the world.'
  • 乃与其妻陈氏同诣贾宅取回 - Final action, meaning 'So he went with his wife Chen to the Jia residence to retrieve her.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 修仪宫 - A palace named 'Palace of Correct Ceremony,' likely a place of ritual importance.
  • 群仙 - Refers to immortals, often associated with Daoist mythology and the afterlife.

  • 丁丑 - A specific year in the Chinese zodiac system, which is used to mark time in a cyclical manner.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • The phrase '至一门，号‘修仪宫’' can be read as 'named 'Palace of Correct Ceremony'' or 'called 'Palace of Correct Ceremony.'
  • The phrase '群仙姑姊亦纷然在侧' can be read as 'gathered around' or 'were gathered around.'
  • The sequence '令生降车辇，拜，揖让升降' can be read as a series of actions: 'ordered the man to dismount, bow, perform courtesy and yielding, and ascend and descend.'
  • The phrase '效欢之礼，颇亦明显' can be read as 'were quite evident' or 'were quite clear.'
  • The phrase '生自尔情义日洽，荣辉日盛' can be read as 'the man's feelings and affection became increasingly harmonious,' and '荣辉日盛' can be read as 'his glory and splendor grew daily.'
  • The phrase '出入车服，游宴宾御，次于王者' can be read as 'comparable to a king' or 'on par with a king.'
  • The phrase '王命生与群僚备武卫，大猎于国西灵龟山' can be read as 'to go on a great hunt in the western part of the country, at Mount Linggu.'
  • The phrase '师徒大获，竟夕而还' can be read as 'the soldiers and their leader achieved great success,' and '竟夕而还' can be read as 'returned at night.'
  • The phrase '生因他日启王曰' can be read as 'the man took another day to speak to the king.'
  • The phrase '臣顷结好之日，大王云奉臣父之命' can be read as 'on the day I formed a friendship,' and '大王云奉臣父之命' can be read as 'the great king said he was following my father's orders.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 放荡者 - Implies a lifestyle that is unconventional and not aligned with societal norms, often associated with a lack of ambition or responsibility.

  • 勝兒若向邊塞彈 - Refers to a performer (勝兒) playing music at the frontier (邊塞).

  • 夢至一處 - '夢' means 'to dream,' '至' means 'to reach,' and '一處' means 'a place.' The phrase indicates that she dreamed of reaching a certain place.

  • 左右廊皆施帷幄 - '左右' means 'left and right,' '廊' means 'corridor,' '皆' means 'all,' '施' means 'to hang,' '帷幄' means 'canopies' or 'tents.' The phrase indicates that canopies are hung in the left and right corridors.
  • 有紫衣吏引張氏於西廊幕次 - '有' means 'there is,' '紫衣' means 'purple robe' (a symbol of high rank), '吏' means 'official,' '引' means 'to lead,' '張氏' means 'the Zhang family,' '西廊' means 'west corridor,' '幕次' means 'tent or enclosure.' The phrase indicates that a high-ranking official led the Zhang family to a tent in the west corridor.

  • 諸女迭助之理泽傅粉 - '諸女' means 'all the young women,' '迭' means 'in turn,' '助' means 'to help,' '之' is a particle, '理泽傅粉' means 'to apply makeup and powder.' The phrase indicates that all the young women helped Zhang apply makeup and powder.

  • 并帅王公也 - '并帅' means 'joint commander,' '王公' means 'Mr. Wang.' The phrase identifies the official as Mr. Wang, the joint commander.

  • 某来 - '某' means 'someone' or 'a certain person,' '来' means 'to come.' The phrase indicates that someone came.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 环梳闹扫学宫妆 - Descriptive phrase likely referring to the preparation or adornment of a scholar or student in a formal or scholarly setting.

  • 何乃出不祥言如是 - Phrase meaning 'Why are you saying such ominous things?' indicating a rebuke or concern.
  • 因卧病累日 - Phrase meaning 'became ill and stayed in bed for several days,' suggesting a prolonged illness.

  • 又有将食来者 - Phrase meaning 'and others brought food,' further emphasizing the care from relatives.
  • 女曰 - Phrase meaning 'the girl said,' indicating a direct speech marker.
  • 且须膏沐澡瀹 - Phrase meaning 'wait for me to anoint, wash, and prepare,' suggesting a ritual or personal grooming.
  • 母听之 - Phrase meaning 'the mother allowed it,' indicating permission.
  • 良久妆盛饰而至 - Phrase meaning 'after a long time, she appeared, fully adorned,' suggesting a period of preparation.
  • 食毕 - Phrase meaning 'after finishing the meal,' indicating the completion of a meal.
  • 乃遍拜父母及坐客 - Phrase meaning 'then she bowed to her parents and the guests,' indicating a gesture of respect or farewell.
  • 时不可留 - Phrase meaning 'time cannot be held back,' suggesting the inevitability of departure or death.
  • 某今往矣 - Phrase meaning 'I am going now,' indicating a final statement before departure.
  • 因援衾而寝 - Phrase meaning 'then she took the quilt and lay down,' suggesting the act of preparing for sleep or death.
  • 父母环伺之 - Phrase meaning 'her parents watched over her,' indicating concern or vigilance.
  • 俄遂卒 - Phrase meaning 'soon she died,' indicating the outcome of the narrative.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 司马才仲 - The name of the author or narrator, likely a historical figure or literary character.
  • 会昌二年六月十五日 - A specific date in the Chinese calendar, indicating the time of the event.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • The relationship between the clauses in phrases such as '环梳闹扫学宫妆，独立闲庭纳夜凉' and '手把玉簪敲砌竹，清歌一曲月如霜' may be unclear, requiring careful interpretation to maintain the original meaning and structure.
  • The relationship between the clauses in phrases such as '谓张曰：“其归辞父母，异日复来。”' and '忽惊啼而寤，手扪衣带曰' may be unclear, requiring careful interpretation to maintain the original meaning and structure.
  • The relationship between the clauses in phrases such as '索笔录之，间其故，泣对所梦' and '吾将死乎？汝梦魇尔，何乃出不祥言如是！' may be unclear, requiring careful interpretation to maintain the original meaning and structure.
  • The relationship between the clauses in phrases such as '因卧病累日，外亲有持酒肴者，又有将食来者' and '女曰：“且须膏沐澡瀹。”母听之' may be unclear, requiring careful interpretation to maintain the original meaning and structure.
  • The relationship between the clauses in phrases such as '良久妆盛饰而至，食毕，乃遍拜父母及坐客' and '时不可留，某今往矣。因援衾而寝' may be unclear, requiring careful interpretation to maintain the original meaning and structure.
  • The relationship between the clauses in phrases such as '父母环伺之，俄遂卒' may be unclear, requiring careful interpretation to maintain the original meaning and structure.

  • 深谷莺啼恨院香 - Poetic expression describing a melancholic atmosphere with orioles in a deep valley and the fragrance of flowers, suggesting longing or sorrow.
  • 偷来花下解珠 - Metaphorical phrase meaning 'to secretly come to the flowers to untie the jade hairpin,' symbolizing a clandestine act of love or desire.
  • 碧云飘断音书绝 - Poetic expression indicating the severance of communication, where '碧云' (blue clouds) symbolize distance and '音书绝' (communication severed) conveys the loss of contact.
  • 空倚玉萧愁凤凰 - Metaphorical phrase meaning 'to vainly lean on a jade flute and grieve for the phoenix,' symbolizing unfulfilled longing or sorrow.
  • 侍卫皆寝，邻近阒然 - Descriptive phrase indicating that all the guards were asleep and the surroundings were quiet and deserted.
  • 姬默然良久，跃下榻，执生手曰 - Narrative phrase describing the lady's silence, her sudden movement to get off the bed, and her taking the man's hand to speak.

  • 又不知郎君高意如何 - Rhetorical question asking 'I don't know what your intentions are.'

  • 磨勒曰：“娘子既坚确如是，此亦小事耳。” - Narrative phrase describing Mr. Mo Le's response, meaning 'Mr. Mo Le said, 'Since the lady is so determined, this is a minor matter.'

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 泊元和八年 - 泊 here likely means 'by' or 'during' the year 813 (the eighth year of the reign of Emperor Xianzong of Tang).
  • 自此寻山水，访至人 - 自此 means 'from this point on.' 寻山水 means 'to seek out mountains and waters,' often used metaphorically to mean seeking seclusion or a spiritual retreat. 访至人 means 'to visit the highest sages' or 'to seek out the most enlightened individuals.'
  • 后渐不知所之 - 渐 means 'gradually.' 不知所之 means 'no longer knew where she was.'
  • 隐娘亦鞭驴而一至京师柩前 - 鞭驴 means 'whipped the donkey' (a common mode of transportation). 一至 means 'once arrived.' 京师 means 'the capital city.' 柩前 means 'before the coffin.'
  • 恸哭而去 - 恸哭 means 'to weep bitterly.' 而去 means 'and then left.'
  • 依前跨白卫如故 - 依前 means 'as before.' 跨白卫 means 'riding a white horse.' 如故 means 'as usual.'
  • 吾药力只保一年患耳 - 吾药力 means 'my medicine's power.' 只保 means 'only protects.' 一年患 means 'for one year's illness.' 耳 is a particle indicating the limit.
  • 隐娘一无所受，但沉醉而去 - 一无所受 means 'did not accept anything.' 但 means 'only.' 沉醉 means 'drunk.' 而去 means 'and left.'

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • 泊元和八年，刘自许人觐，隐娘不愿从焉 — This sentence introduces the setting and the main characters. 隐娘不愿从焉 can be read as 隐娘 did not wish to accompany.
  • 自此寻山水，访至人，但一一请给与其夫 — This sentence describes the actions of the characters. 但一一请给与其夫 can be read as but requested provisions for her husband.
  • 后渐不知所之 — This sentence indicates a change in the situation. 渐不知所之 can be read as gradually no longer knew where she was.
  • 及刘薨于军，隐娘亦鞭驴而一至京师柩前，恸哭而去 — This sentence describes the aftermath of Liu's death. 隐娘亦鞭驴而一至京师柩前 can be read as 隐娘 also whipped the donkey and once arrived at the coffin in the capital city.
